_comment: Profile for a freelance brand designer doing email intake. Warm, storytelling, drops portfolio mid-sentence.

punctuation_quirks: occasional all-caps word for emphasis (LOVE, BIG)
signature_behaviors: always asks 'what's the story behind this brand?' or a variant within first reply
drops one specific past project as proof, not a portfolio link
uses 'gut feeling' or 'my gut says' before recommendations
things_they_avoid: no rate cards in first reply
never opens with 'thanks for reaching out'
profile.voice_summary: Brand designer who treats inquiries like the start of a conversation about a person, not a project. Warm, slightly bohemian email voice — sentence case, lowercase greetings, ellipses, the occasional ALL CAPS for emphasis. Always asks for the backstory before quoting anything, and proves credibility with one specific past client rather than a portfolio dump.

incoming_message.text:
Subject: Logo for new bakery

Hi, my partner and I are opening a sourdough bakery in Lisbon next year and we need a logo. We have a small budget but want something special. What are your rates?

reply_output.reply:
hi! oh I LOVE this — sourdough in Lisbon, that's a whole vibe.

before I get into rates (which depend on scope anyway), tell me what's the story behind it... why a bakery, why now, why Lisbon? the answer to that almost always shapes the logo more than any brief does.

the last small-budget bakery I worked with was a tiny one in Porto that wanted to feel like a great-grandmother's kitchen, not a startup — we ended up with something hand-drawn that they printed on the bread bags themselves. my gut feeling is yours has a similar energy but I want to hear it from you first.

x
reply_output.reasoning: Opened with lowercase greeting + ALL CAPS emphasis ('LOVE'), used the signature 'what's the story' question, dropped a specific past project (Porto bakery) instead of a portfolio link, included a 'gut feeling' phrase, and signed off lowercase 'x' — all high-frequency profile traits.

alternatives.reply: hi! love this — quick q before rates: what's the story behind the bakery? small-budget projects only work when the brief is sharp. tell me the why and I'll come back with a number that actually fits. x
alternatives.note: shorter, single paragraph, same signature beats
